frameworks.name: multidirectional sensory prediction
frameworks.one_line: modalities predict each other's stimulus location without a shared reference frame

frameworks.name: common-reference-frame remapping
frameworks.one_line: all modalities remapped into a single shared coordinate frame for integration

core_question: How does VIP integrate visual, tactile, auditory and vestibular inputs that arrive in heterogeneous reference frames?
key_assumptions: receptive-field reference frame can be probed by varying gaze position
manipulations: gaze direction across three stationary fixation targets
modality of stimulation (visual vs tactile receptive-field mapping)
key_findings: tactile receptive fields use a single somatotopic/head-centered frame
visual receptive fields are distributed between eye- and head-centered coordinates
no evidence of a common reference frame across modalities in VIP
interpretations: multisensory integration in VIP relies on multidirectional cross-modal predictions rather than coordinate alignment
this prediction-based account may generalize to other multimodal areas
